title: The Fire That Refused to Burn: Prophet Ibrahim (AS)

sound.ambient: deep fire crackling ambience, low roar of flames, embers popping, warm and tense, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Cinematic Quran story - Prophet Ibrahim (AS) and the fire (Surah al-Anbiya 21:51-70). Real aniconic PORTRAIT fire footage via Pexels. Six verses auto-pulled AND recited (AbdulBasit Murattal, reciter 2) while each ayah holds. Honorific: voice says 'peace be upon him', screen shows '(AS)' - both = alayhis-salam, never the Prophet-Muhammad-only form. Dramatic tone. Shop-waitlist closer. No em dashes.

segments.title:
The Story of
Prophet Ibrahim
segments.text: This is the story of Prophet Ibrahim, peace be upon him, and the fire that was commanded not to burn him.
segments.caption: Prophet Ibrahim (AS)

segments.text: Ibrahim watched his people bow down to statues they had carved with their own hands. And he asked the question that no one else dared to ask.
segments.caption: Why worship what you built?

segments.text: He said to his father and his people: what are these statues that you stand before in devotion?
segments.caption: Surah al-Anbiya · 21:52

segments.text: They had no answer, only the way of their fathers. So Ibrahim waited. And when they left for their festival, he made his move.
segments.caption: He had a plan.

segments.text: He broke them all to pieces, except the largest of them, so that they might come back to it.
segments.caption: Surah al-Anbiya · 21:58

segments.text: When they returned to find their gods shattered on the ground, their fury turned to a single name. They dragged Ibrahim before the crowd and demanded answers.
segments.caption: Who did this?

segments.text: He said: rather, it was this, the largest of them. So ask them, if they are able to speak.
segments.caption: Ask them yourself.

segments.text: And for one moment, they saw the truth. Their gods could not speak, could not move, could not even defend themselves. So they buried that truth, and they reached for fire.
segments.caption: Stone cannot save you.

segments.text: They said: burn him, and support your gods, if you are going to act.
segments.caption: Surah al-Anbiya · 21:68

segments.text: They gathered wood for days, and lit a blaze so vast that no one could stand near it. They threw him in. And in that moment, the command went not to an angel, not to a prophet, but to the fire itself.
segments.caption: Then God spoke to the fire.

segments.text: We said: O fire, be coolness and safety upon Ibrahim.
segments.caption: O fire, be cool.

segments.text: The flames that should have devoured him turned cool and gentle around him. He sat in the very heart of the fire, untouched.
segments.caption: Cool. And safe.

segments.text: And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers.
segments.caption: Surah al-Anbiya · 21:70

segments.text: One man, alone, with nothing but his faith. And the fire could not touch a single hair on his head. Because nothing in this world can harm you, except by the permission of the One who made it.
segments.caption: Allah was enough for him.

segments.text: Comment which prophet's story you want me to tell next.
segments.caption: Which story next?

data.headline: Something beautiful is coming
data.sub: Personalized storybooks, photo keepsakes, and cards sealed with a dua.

segments.text: And something beautiful is coming from Ketabi Studio. Personalized storybooks, keepsakes, and cards, each one sealed with a prayer. Join the founding list now, at Ketabi Studio dot com.

sources: Surah al-Anbiya 21:51-70 - Ibrahim questions the idols, breaks them (except the largest), is sentenced to the fire, and Allah commands it to be cool and safe
Quran 21:52 ('What are these statues to which you are devoted?') - auto-pulled + recited
Quran 21:58 (broke them to pieces except the largest, that they might return to it) - auto-pulled + recited
Quran 21:63 ('Rather, this - the largest of them - did it, so ask them, if they should speak') - auto-pulled + recited
Quran 21:68 ('Burn him and support your gods, if you are to act') - auto-pulled + recited
Quran 21:69 ('O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham') - auto-pulled + recited
Quran 21:70 ('And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers') - auto-pulled + recited

claims.claim: Ibrahim asked his people about the statues they were devoted to
He broke the idols to pieces except the largest, that they might return to it
He told them to ask the largest idol, if it could speak (exposing their powerlessness)
They said: 'Burn him and support your gods, if you are to act'
Allah said: 'O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham'
'They intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers'
Nothing harms a person except by Allah's permission (reflection)
